result.vlm_summary:
page_state:
Apple homepage, scrolled to the iPhone hero section; no pricing or AirTag content visible.
task_relevant_visible_text:
Apple; Store; Mac; iPad; iPhone; Watch; Vision; AirPods; TV & Home; Entertainment; Accessories; Support; iPhone; Say hello to the latest generation of iPhone.; Learn more; Shop iPhone; iPad air
selected_candidate:
The visible “Learn more” button in the iPhone hero section
constraint_evidence:
- latest model of Apple AirTag: not_visible
- pricing: not_visible
- availability: not_visible
- bulk purchase options: not_visible
- on Apple’s website: supported (URL/title/context show apple.com / Apple)
- product is AirTag specifically: contradicted (visible content is iPhone, not AirTag)
action_target:
Click the “Learn more” link/button in the iPhone hero area
success_signals:
Navigation to an iPhone product/learn-more page after click
failure_signals:
Still on homepage or navigates to iPhone content unrelated to AirTag pricing/availability; no AirTag/bulk purchase info appears
uncertainty:
The click may open an iPhone detail page, but the screenshot does not show the destination yet.

args.task: navigate: Check the pricing and availability of the latest model of Apple AirTag, including bulk purchase options on Apple's website.

args.query: Apple task AirTag pricing availability bulk purchase wrong product clicked iPhone learn more instead of AirTag

evidence.claim: The task required checking pricing and availability for the latest Apple AirTag model, including bulk purchase options.
At step 23, the high-detail screenshot shows the agent clicking the iPhone hero section's 'Learn more' link on apple.com, not any AirTag content.

The high-detail step summary explicitly says AirTag, pricing, availability, and bulk purchase options were not visible, and the visible content contradicted the task by showing iPhone instead of AirTag.
After the click, the trajectory only reaches the iPhone page and ends with an exit message; no AirTag pricing or bulk-purchase page is reached.
No validated similar-success run or prior eval case was retrieved to suggest this was a known acceptable path.

args.expected_behavior: Navigate to Apple AirTag product information and inspect pricing, availability, and any bulk purchase options for the latest model.
args.actual_behavior: The agent clicked a 'Learn more' link in the iPhone hero section and moved to the iPhone page, never showing any AirTag pricing, availability, or bulk purchase information.
args.regression_rule: If the task asks for AirTag pricing/availability, do not click generic homepage hero links that lead to iPhone or other products; navigate directly to the AirTag product page or search for AirTag-specific content before stopping.

suggested_followups.label: Need AirTag page
suggested_followups.message: Would you like me to trace where the AirTag product page should have been reached from the Apple site?

suggested_followups.label: Compare success path
suggested_followups.message: Should I look for a successful AirTag navigation path to compare against this run?
